test_scripts.generation_notes: This is a highly complex test case requiring printer enrollment, subscription state management, time shifting via Gemini Rails Admin, print job simulation via RTP device status, and comprehensive UI verification of progress bars, tooltips, and page usage tracking. The test uses existing framework components for enrollment, Gemini RA operations, and print simulation. New locators were created for Billing Cycle Period card elements including plan pause information, complimentary pages progress bar with tooltip, additional pages progress bar with tooltip, and info icons. The test follows all framework conventions including proper error handling, logging, and Playwright expect() assertions. Print job simulation uses send_rtp_devicestatus() from test_flows_common. Time shifting uses GeminiRAHelper.event_shift(). All preconditions are properly handled including account creation, printer enrollment with 50+ pages plan, subscription state verification, and plan pause activation.

all_existing_files_to_update_in_framewor: pages/print_history_page.py (merge print_history_page_additions.py - add 13 new locators to Elements class)
pages/overview_page.py (merge overview_page_additions.py - add 5 new locators to Elements class)
integration_instructions: 1. Clone the AAVA-STORAGE repository: git clone https://github.com/Roykunaljit/AAVA-STORAGE.git
2. Navigate to test_scripts/ folder and locate billing_cycle_period_card.py
3. In AURA-FRAMEWORK repository, create folder: test_flows/BillingCyclePeriodCard/
4. Copy billing_cycle_period_card.py to test_flows/BillingCyclePeriodCard/
5. Open pages/print_history_page.py in AURA-FRAMEWORK
6. Locate the Elements class inside PrintHistoryPage
7. Add all 13 new locators from print_history_page_additions.py to the Elements class
8. Open pages/overview_page.py in AURA-FRAMEWORK
9. Locate the Elements class inside OverviewPage
10. Add all 5 new locators from overview_page_additions.py to the Elements class
11. Verify all imports are available in AURA-FRAMEWORK
12. Run the test: pytest test_flows/BillingCyclePeriodCard/billing_cycle_period_card.py
13. IMPORTANT: This test requires backend infrastructure including:
- Virtual printer creation via G2 simulator API
- Gemini Rails Admin access for time shifting and subscription management
- RTP device status API for print job simulation
- Enrollment flow completion
14. Ensure test environment has all required API endpoints configured
15. Test execution time: Approximately 10-15 minutes due to enrollment and time shifting operations
16. The test creates a fresh account, enrolls a printer with 50 pages plan, pauses the plan, shifts time by 32 days, simulates printing, and verifies all UI elements on the Billing Cycle Period card

reasons: Requires complete printer enrollment flow with 50+ pages plan selection
Requires Gemini Rails Admin access for subscription state management and time shifting
Requires print job simulation via RTP device status API
Requires time shifting by 32 days to trigger billing cycle
Requires verification of multiple progress bars with specific colors (black for complimentary, yellow for additional)
Requires tooltip interactions with hover/click based on device type
Requires verification of dynamic page usage values that change after printing
Involves 21 test steps with complex state dependencies
Requires visual verification of progress bar fill colors
Requires responsive testing considerations (desktop vs mobile/tablet interactions)
prerequisites: G2 simulator API access for virtual printer creation
Gemini Rails Admin access for subscription management
RTP device status API for print simulation
Time shifting capability in test environment
special_considerations: Print job simulation uses send_rtp_devicestatus() which updates impression counts in binary payload
Time shifting uses GeminiRAHelper.event_shift() with force_billing=True
Progress bar color verification may require CSS property checking or visual comparison
Tooltip interactions are device-specific (hover for desktop, click for mobile/tablet)
Page refresh is required after print simulation to see updated values
Test creates real subscription data that may need cleanup
automation_challenges: Backend time manipulation is not standard UI automation
Print job simulation requires special API access
Progress bar color verification (black vs yellow) may be fragile
Multiple page refreshes with data synchronization waits
Complex precondition setup with multiple dependencies
